texto: I have the impression that our children are much more excited about going to school than children in other countries are. They think of it as a special privilege. Going to school, being with other children, getting books and pencils - all of that is like a dream for them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cheryl Benard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “I have the impression that our children are much more excited about going to school than children…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, afghanistan, education pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de education e privilege.
